title: Small-System Architecture, Data Boundaries, and Decision Records · Product Forensics
summary: A preserved decision record for small-system architecture, data boundaries, and decision records contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in bounded architecture and data contract packet and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood.
Separate domain decisions from interface, persistence, network, clock, identity, and third-party effects through narrow owned boundaries.
Model identities, states, relationships, invariants, invalid cases, provenance, and lifecycle before choosing storage representation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Driver
steps.instruction:
debug case — Framework handlers mutate shared records and call storage and services directly, making one changed rule require edits across the product. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood.

Inspect the Architecture Driver trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Driver.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Driver, keep Architecture Driver connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Architecture Driver, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Driver, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Architecture Driver precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Driver, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: Framework handlers mutate shared records and call storage and services directly, making one changed rule require edits across the product. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Architecture Driver, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Using a fashionable pattern is an architecture rationale.

stimulus.title: Architecture Driver case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Framework handlers mutate shared records and call storage and services directly, making one changed rule require edits across the product. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Using a fashionable pattern is an architecture rationale.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive architecture from user flows, quality attributes, risk, data sensitivity, team capacity, and change likelihood. Artifact under review: bounded architecture and data contract packet.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Architecture Driver, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Decision Record
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-decision-record-yfuv0p: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Decision Record.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Decision Record into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Decision Record, keep Decision Record connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
pp1-decision-record-yfuv0p:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Decision Record
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Decision Record: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Framework handlers mutate shared records and call storage and services directly, making one changed rule require edits across the product. The debug variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Record context, forces, alternatives, decision, consequences, evidence, review trigger, and supersession for consequential choices.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Decision Record.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Decision Record, keep Decision Record connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record context, forces, alternatives, decision, consequences, evidence, review trigger, and supersession for consequential choices.
